title: Best Supporting Actress: Post-Golden Globes Oscar Analysis
text: Current Frontrunner: Teyana Taylor has taken the lead after winning the Golden Globe for her role in One Battle After Another. She displaced Amy Madigan, who held the top position just days earlier following her Critics Choice Awards victory. Taylor's Momentum: In her acceptance speech, Taylor emphasized representation, stating: our voices matter and our dreams deserve space. This powerful moment resonated with voters and reset the competitive landscape. The Screen Time Question: Both leading contenders face a common challenge—limited on-screen presence. Taylor appears for approximately 19 minutes in her film, while Madigan has roughly 14 minutes in Weapons. However, as recent awards success demonstrates, performance impact transcends duration. Current Odds: Teyana Taylor: 97.8%, Amy Madigan: 95.9%, Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas: 92.0%. The race remains competitive, with both frontrunners virtually locked for nominations despite their supporting roles essentially functioning as co-leads within their respective narratives.
